_: Reference cases for the FCCAM (AM skywave) sidecar.
_sourcing: Each case is a (transmitter, receiver, expected 50% skywave field) tuple drawn
from FCC-published nighttime allocation data — the same source consulting firms
(H&D, du Treil-Lundin-Rackley, Cohen Dippell) cite when filing §73.182 exhibits.
Suggested sources, in order of preference:
1) FCC's published nighttime contour data for Class I-A clear-channel stations
(WLW 700, WSM 650, WBZ 1030 — well-documented historical NIF radii).
2) FCC Allocations Tables in §73.183 Figure 1 reproductions.
3) Cross-validation against FCC's own AM Query online tool nighttime field
Each `expected_field_uv_m` should be the value FCCAM produces on the FCC's own
build of the same Fccam.for source.  Do NOT use V-Soft AM-Pro or H&D's internal
tools as the reference — those are downstream consumers of the same FORTRAN.
Until the sidecar is up + at least 5 of these cases pass, AM nighttime exhibits
tolerance_basis: FCCAM's stated output precision is ~1 µV/m; we tolerate 5 to absorb great-circle distance + midpoint-lat rounding at the Genoa side without spurious mismatches.

schema.freq_khz: carrier frequency in kHz, on the 10-kHz US AM grid
schema.rx_lat: receiver latitude (deg) — picks a path that exercises a particular skywave-curve regime
schema.midpoint_lat: (optional) great-circle midpoint latitude; client computes if omitted
schema.expected_field_uv_m: expected 50% skywave field strength at the receiver from FCC published data
schema.expected_source: URL / citation for the expected value (FCC document, AM Query screenshot, etc.)
